title: The first hello
summary: One conversation became another, and somehow the world had started to feel different.
story: This space is waiting for Nahuel's real memory of the first conversation—the words, the setting, and the tiny detail that Wasiatus will recognize immediately.
The finished story should sound like Nahuel speaking directly to her, not like a formal biography.
quote: Some beginnings are quiet. Their echoes last forever.

title: All the days between
summary: The jokes no one else understood. The conversations that lasted too long. The first collection of our favorite ordinary days.
story: Add two or three true moments here: a first date detail, a ridiculous shared joke, or the day Nahuel realized this was becoming something permanent.
Small specificity will make this chapter more emotional than any generic romantic phrase.
quote: You made ordinary feel like something worth remembering.

title: The promise we keep
summary: Not only one beautiful day, but every choice that followed it.
story: Replace this placeholder with the wedding memory Wasiatus would choose first: what Nahuel saw, felt, or almost forgot to say.
Then connect that day to the quieter promise of choosing each other in daily life.
quote: I chose you then. I choose you still.

title: Hello, Rayden
summary: Our love grew hands to hold, a laugh to recognize, and a whole new future to imagine.
story: Tell the real story of pregnancy and Rayden's birth here, centered on what Nahuel admired in Wasiatus and how it felt to meet Rayden.
This chapter can include a message to Wasiatus as a mother and a small moment that captures the family's everyday joy.
quote: The two of us became a family, and love learned a new language.

title: A new map of home
summary: New streets, new seasons, and the same three people learning how to call another place home.
story: Add the real details of moving to Japan: the courage it took, the surprises, the difficult parts, and the family moments now impossible to imagine living without.
Photos from different seasons can turn this into a visual passage through the life you built together.
quote: Home was never only a place. It was us.

title: All our tomorrows
summary: More birthdays, more adventures, more laughter from the next room—and more reasons to choose one another.
story: Write three specific dreams here: one simple, one adventurous, and one about the kind of family life Nahuel hopes to keep building with Wasiatus.
End in the present—with gratitude for who she is at thirty-five, not only for the memories already made.
quote: The best thing about the future is finding you in it.
